steps.title: Identify the key action in context
steps.content: The passage says the principal chief and cabinet officials do something to laws by devising policies and administering services in accordance with them. That phrase tells us exactly what they do: they turn laws into concrete action through policies and services. The blank needs a word meaning to put laws into effect.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'implement': means to put into effect or carry out — perfectly matches “devising policies and administering services in accordance with” the laws. ✓
  • Choice B — 'presume': means to assume something based on incomplete information — nothing in the passage suggests the chief and cabinet are making assumptions about the laws. ✗
  • Choice C — 'improvise': means to create without preparation — the passage describes deliberate policy-making, the opposite of improvising. ✗
  • Choice D — 'mimic': means to copy or imitate — the chief and cabinet are not generating imitation laws; they are executing existing ones. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
